session_summary: Pivoted away from the Telegram channels plugin entirely. The plugin was gated on the GrowthBook flag tengu_harbor and the inbound channel feature never worked across two restart cycles. Live research surfaced Anthropic's official Claude Code Remote Control (shipped Feb 2026), which does exactly what John wanted — drive his local Claude Code session from the Claude mobile app or claude.ai/code — with one command and no plugin/flag dependency. Uninstalled all Telegram artifacts (plugin cache, channel dir, settings entries, killed bun processes). Two memory lessons captured: (1) research alternatives even when John has proposed an approach, (2) don't stop short of completing a directive with menu-style options.
immediate_task: John needs to exit this Claude Code session and relaunch with `claude --rc` (or `claude --remote-control`). The in-session slash command `/remote-control` is BROKEN on 2.1.x — confirmed via GitHub issues #29265, #30020, #33327, #46345 across versions 2.1.62/2.1.80/2.1.100 and John just confirmed 'unknown command' on 2.1.138. The CLI flag is reliable. After relaunch, the terminal prints a session URL and QR code; John scans with the Claude mobile app to pair. If he doesn't have the mobile app, `/mobile` inside the new session shows a download QR.

session_lessons: GrowthBook tengu_harbor channel feature was still failing even with the cache showing tengu_harbor=true — likely a separate gating check we never identified. Moot now since we abandoned the channels approach.
Anthropic shipped Claude Code Remote Control in Feb 2026 — it's the supported path for driving a local session from a phone. No plugin/flag dependency.
Before implementing any user-proposed infrastructure or tooling decision, do a 1-2 search research pass for officially-supported alternatives (memory: feedback_research_user_proposed_paths.md).
When John gives a directive, execute it fully including obvious adjacent cleanup — no option menus, no 'should I also' asks (memory: feedback_dont_stop_short.md).
